identificationModule.officialTitle: A Randomized Controlled Trial of RISE for Nurse Leaders

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to determine whether RISE for Nurse Leaders has a significant impact on nurse leaders' post-traumatic growth, resilience, insight, self-compassion, and empowerment, as well as mental well-being, in their personal lives and their working environment.

armGroups.label: Intervention group
armGroups.description: Intervention group will attend the RISE for Nurse Leaders program, which consists of nine 90-minute weekly psychoeducational group sessions facilitated by a licensed mental health counselor (LMHC)
interventionNames: Behavioral: RISE for Nurse Leaders Program

armGroups.label: Wait-list control group
armGroups.description: Control group will attend the RISE for Nurse Leaders program after the 3-month wait-list period
interventionNames: Behavioral: RISE for Nurse Leaders Program

interventions.name: RISE for Nurse Leaders Program
interventions.description: Psychoeducational sessions designed to impact resilience, insight, self-compassion, and empowerment

primaryOutcomes.description: 21 item questionnaire with a scale of 0-5, with 0 meaning I did not experience this change as a result of my crisis; and 5 meaning I experienced this change to a very great degree as a result of my crisis
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Brief Index of Affective Job Satisfaction
secondaryOutcomes.description: 4 item questionnaire with a scale of 1-5, with 1 meaning strongly disagree; and 5 meaning strongly agree
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.description: 6 item questionnaire with a scale of 1-5, with 1 meaning strongly disagree; and 5 meaning strongly agree
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.description: 10 item questionnaire with a scale of 1-4, with 1 meaning not at all true; and 4 meaning exactly true
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.description: 10 item questionnaire with a scale of 0-4, with 0 meaning never; and 4 meaning very often
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.description: 30 item questionnaire with a scale of 1-5 with 1 meaning never; and 5 meaning very often
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.description: 12 item questionnaire with a scale of A-G, with A meaning very strongly disagree; and G meaning very strongly agree
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

secondaryOutcomes.description: 20 item questionnaire with a scale of 1-6; with 1 meaning strongly disagree; and 6 meaning strongly agree
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Group comparison from baseline to 3-month follow-up

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Adult ≥ 18 years old
* Licensed as an RN
* Unit-based nurse leader (i.e., assistant nurse manager, nurse manager) employed by AdventHealth in a hospital-based setting at the Altamonte Springs, Apopka, Celebration, Daytona Beach, DeLand, East Orlando, Fish Memorial, Kissimmee, New Smyrna Beach, Orlando, Palm Coast, Waterman, or Winter Park campus
* Able to speak, read, and understand English fluently
* Able to provide informed consent
* Willing and able to comply with all study procedures and requirements for the duration of the study.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Employed as a direct care nurse or in another level of nursing leadership (i.e., director of nursing, executive leader)
* A study participant of IRBNet #1504917 titled A Pilot Study of RISE for Nurse Managers
